mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, forcing equity repricing and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles correlate with positive 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC > 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.3% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank intervention, causing immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market absorption, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings growth to drive positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth deceleration indicates macroeconomic demand exhaustion, prompting institutional deleveraging and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines in recovery phases yield positive 20-day SPX returns with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production dropping below its 6-month moving average signals acute supply chain bottlenecks or demand collapse, triggering algorithmic risk reduction and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when INDPRO crosses below its 6-month MA.
